lines.target: Now that we have learned how to become rich, let's look at some expressions concerning money and business.
lines.base: Ahora que hemos aprendido cómo hacernos ricos, observemos algunas expresiones relacionadas con el dinero y los negocios.

lines.target: Over the Christmas period, the major stores do a roaring trade in consumer goods.
lines.base: Durante el período de Navidad, los grandes almacenes hacen un gran negocio con los bienes de consumo.

lines.target: Despite a general business slump, electrical household goods are selling like hot cakes.
lines.base: A pesar de un marasmo generalizado en los negocios, los electrodomésticos se venden como «rosquillas».

lines.target: Unfortunately, another booming industry at this time of year is shop-lifting...
lines.base: Desgraciadamente, otra industria en alza en esta época del año es la de robar.

lines.target: Consumer trends are changing. More and more people are turning away from the High Street
lines.base: Las tendencias de los consumidores cambian. Más y más personas se alejan de los comercios de High Street

lines.target: and doing their shopping in supermarkets, hypermarkets or huge cash-and-carry warehouses.
lines.base: y hacen sus compras en supermercados, hipermercados o en enormes almacenes cash-and-carry de comercio mayorista.

lines.target: How ever did you afford the new car? — The never-never, old man. I didn't have the ready cash so I bought it on HP.
lines.base: ¿Cómo has podido pagar el coche nuevo? — Con el nunca-jamás, viejo. No tenía el efectivo, así que lo compré a plazos (HP).

lines.target: A landscape in oils by Turner went under the hammer in New York last night. It fetched a record price of $30,720,000.
lines.base: Un paisaje al óleo de Turner se vendió en subasta en Nueva York anoche. Alcanzó un precio récord de 30.720.000 dólares.

lines.target: Now is the time to invest in blue chip stock. The market is still bullish but we are promised a bearish trend in the next few days.
lines.base: Ahora es el momento de invertir en acciones preferentes. El mercado está todavía alcista, pero se prevé una tendencia bajista en los próximos días.

lines.target: That new computer store in Market Street has just moved into new premises. They must be making a bomb!
lines.base: Esa nueva tienda de informática en Market Street se ha mudado a unos locales nuevos. ¡Deben estar ganando una fortuna!

lines.target: The saloon model is within your price-range but you'll pay through the nose if you want the sports version.
lines.base: El modelo berlina está dentro de tu presupuesto, pero pagarás un precio exorbitante por la versión deportiva.

lines.target: I got ripped off when I bought my compact disc player. He charged me well over the odds.
lines.base: Me estafaron cuando compré mi reproductor de CD. Me cobró por encima de lo normal.

lines.target: Well I wouldn't buy one just now. They cost an arm and a leg.
lines.base: Bueno, yo no compraría uno ahora mismo. Cuestan un ojo de la cara.

lines.target: There was a small dent in the casing, so the salesman knocked off 10 %.
lines.base: Había un pequeño defecto en la carcasa, así que el vendedor me hizo un 10 % de descuento.

lines.target: He's a lousy manager and yet his company's finances seem in good shape. — Yes, he's got an intelligent sleeping partner.
lines.base: Es un mal gestor, y sin embargo las finanzas de su empresa parecen sanas. — Sí, tiene un socio capitalista inteligente.

lines.target: That blender I bought from Marshall's broke down three times in a month. I got a real pig in a poke.
lines.base: Esa batidora que compré en Marshall's se averió tres veces en un mes. Me vendieron gato por liebre.

lines.target: That new bistrot in St. James' Square is fantastic — but don't go there unless you're well-heeled. It costs a mint.
lines.base: Ese nuevo bistró en la Plaza St. James es fantástico, pero no vayas a menos que tengas dinero. ¡Dejarás hasta la camisa!

lines.target: A company director was advised by his bank manager that he had an overdraft of £6,000 and that interest would be 22 % per annum.
lines.base: Un director de empresa fue informado por su banquero que tenía un descubierto de 6000 libras y que el interés sería del 22 % anual.

lines.target: The client insisted that he should deposit his Rolls Royce as security. He drove it to the bank and left it in the car park.
lines.base: El cliente insistió en dejar su Rolls Royce como garantía. Lo llevó al banco y lo dejó en el aparcamiento.

lines.target: When he returned to pick up the car a month later, the bank manager asked him why he had insisted on leaving the car as security.
lines.base: Cuando volvió a recogerlo un mes después, el director del banco le preguntó por qué había insistido en dejar el coche como garantía.

lines.target: Well I was off on holiday, came the reply, and where else can you get a month's free parking in Central London?
lines.base: Bueno, estaba de vacaciones, fue la respuesta, ¿y en qué otro sitio puedes aparcar gratis un mes en el centro de Londres?

gap_candidates.target: Over the Christmas period, the major stores do a roaring trade in consumer goods.
Despite a general business slump, electrical household goods are selling like hot cakes.
Unfortunately, another booming industry at this time of year is shop-lifting...
Consumer trends are changing. More and more people are turning away from the High Street
and doing their shopping in supermarkets, hypermarkets or huge cash-and-carry warehouses.
How ever did you afford the new car? — The never-never, old man. I didn't have the ready cash so I bought it on HP.
A landscape in oils by Turner went under the hammer in New York last night. It fetched a record price of $30,720,000.
Now is the time to invest in blue chip stock. The market is still bullish but we are promised a bearish trend in the next few days.
That new computer store in Market Street has just moved into new premises. They must be making a bomb!
The saloon model is within your price-range but you'll pay through the nose if you want the sports version.
I got ripped off when I bought my compact disc player. He charged me well over the odds.
Well I wouldn't buy one just now. They cost an arm and a leg.
There was a small dent in the casing, so the salesman knocked off 10 %.
That blender I bought from Marshall's broke down three times in a month. I got a real pig in a poke.
That new bistrot in St. James' Square is fantastic — but don't go there unless you're well-heeled. It costs a mint.
A company director was advised by his bank manager that he had an overdraft of £6,000 and that interest would be 22 % per annum.
The client insisted that he should deposit his Rolls Royce as security. He drove it to the bank and left it in the car park.
When he returned to pick up the car a month later, the bank manager asked him why he had insisted on leaving the car as security.
Well I was off on holiday, came the reply, and where else can you get a month's free parking in Central London?
